common.search: Search goals, customers, runs, or capabilities

sectionDescriptions.command: Set direction, govern decisions, and see whether the complete growth system is producing measurable business outcomes.
sectionDescriptions.brand: Maintain the shared business, product, market, customer, knowledge, evidence, and asset context used across Grovello.
sectionDescriptions.content: Plan, create, optimize, and publish governed content experiences that earn discoverability and qualified traffic.
sectionDescriptions.channels: Connect owned, social, partner, and paid channels while keeping identity, policy, spend, and performance accountable.
sectionDescriptions.pipeline: Discover and qualify target accounts, coordinate compliant outreach, and manage conversations in one customer context.
sectionDescriptions.revenue: Move qualified demand through pipeline, sales collaboration, commercial agreements, billing, payment, and recognized revenue.
sectionDescriptions.customer: Turn onboarding and adoption into durable customer value, retention, expansion, advocacy, and referrals.
sectionDescriptions.data: Unify growth and customer data, convert market signals into intelligence, and measure attribution, performance, and experiments.
sectionDescriptions.automation: Operate durable workflows, specialized agents, integrations, model routing, and developer extension contracts.
sectionDescriptions.governance: Manage workspaces, access, policy, consent, security, auditability, and system-wide operating settings.

dashboard.title: Growth Overview
dashboard.description: See revenue progress, active growth loops, decisions, risks, and execution health in one place.

journeys.title: Growth Journeys
journeys.description: Configure, operate, and verify complete paths from market entry and demand through revenue, retention, and the next strategy cycle.

deliveryRoadmap.title: Delivery Roadmap
deliveryRoadmap.description: Track the fourteen dependency-ordered product stages, required deliverables, and evidence-based completion gates.

architecture.title: System Architecture
architecture.description: Understand how shared context, agents, workflows, integrations, channels, and revenue feedback form one operating system.

goals.title: Goals & Budgets
goals.description: Define measurable revenue goals, operating constraints, market priorities, and approved budgets.

decisions.title: Decisions & Approvals
decisions.description: Review evidence-backed recommendations, risks, resource changes, and high-impact actions under explicit approval policy.

plans.title: Strategy & Plans
plans.description: Turn go-to-market strategy into sequenced initiatives, owners, milestones, dependencies, and measurable outcomes.

brandRules.title: Business & Brand
brandRules.description: Maintain business identity, positioning, voice, terminology, visual rules, claims, exclusions, and market-specific guidance.

products.title: Products, Offers & Pricing
products.description: Manage products, solutions, price books, pricing boundaries, proof, certifications, and commercial packages.

markets.title: Markets & Localization
markets.description: Define domestic and international markets, regions, languages, currencies, regulations, channel availability, and market-entry assumptions.

icp.title: Ideal Customer Profiles
icp.description: Define target accounts, buying committees, pains, triggers, qualification rules, and exclusions.

knowledge.title: Knowledge & Evidence
knowledge.description: Ground agents in verified documents, facts, FAQs, case studies, policies, claims, evidence, and citations.

assets.title: Asset Library
assets.description: Upload governed images and documents, track clean immutable versions, and request secure downloads.

businessSetup.title: Business Setup
businessSetup.description: Complete, review, and activate the canonical business profile that governs downstream growth work.

imports.title: Imports
imports.description: Upload, map, validate, approve, apply, cancel, and compensate structured business-truth imports.

contentFactory.title: Content Studio
contentFactory.description: Plan and produce governed multilingual content variants for every audience, market, and channel.

sites.title: Sites & Landing Pages
sites.description: Build websites, landing pages, campaign hubs, resource centers, forms, and conversion paths.

seo.title: Search Optimization (SEO)
seo.description: Discover demand, build topic clusters, publish pages, manage internal links, and measure revenue impact.

geo.title: AI Search (GEO)
geo.description: Improve visibility in AI-generated answers through governed questions, claims, citations, entities, and referral measurement.

video.title: Video
video.description: Orchestrate scripts, storyboards, voice, captions, media, rendering, review, and delivery.

publish.title: Publishing
publish.description: Schedule, approve, distribute, retry, and audit content across connected channels.

channelAccounts.title: Channel Connections
channelAccounts.description: Manage channel identities, credentials, permissions, health, quotas, territories, and policies.

social.title: Social Media
social.description: Coordinate calendars, conversations, community actions, response policies, and performance.

ads.title: Paid Media
ads.description: Manage media strategy, audiences, creative tests, budgets, guardrails, and revenue return.

leads.title: Lead Intelligence
leads.description: Find, enrich, verify, qualify, deduplicate, and source target accounts and contacts compliantly.

outbound.title: Outreach Sequences
outbound.description: Coordinate consent-aware email and multichannel account outreach with deliverability and risk controls.

inbox.title: Unified Inbox
inbox.description: Merge channel conversations into one customer timeline for triage, response, and follow-up.

crm.title: CRM & Pipeline
crm.description: Maintain shared customer records, relationship activity, opportunities, forecasts, and next actions.

sales.title: Sales Workspace
sales.description: Coordinate meetings, questions, recommendations, next steps, and human-owned selling decisions.

commercial.title: Quote-to-Cash
commercial.description: Govern proposals, discounts, contracts, orders, invoices, collections, payments, and revenue events.

success.title: Onboarding & Success
success.description: Coordinate onboarding, adoption, value milestones, health signals, risks, and customer outcomes.

retention.title: Health, Retention & Renewal
retention.description: Monitor customer health, forecast renewal, detect churn risk, activate playbooks, and measure retained revenue.

referral.title: Expansion & Advocacy
referral.description: Identify cross-sell, upsell, customer advocacy, partner, and referral opportunities.

dataCenter.title: Unified Data
dataCenter.description: Govern canonical identities, events, metrics, lineage, quality, synchronization, imports, and exports.

attribution.title: Revenue Attribution
attribution.description: Connect spend and touchpoints to pipeline, orders, revenue, retention, and attribution confidence.

reports.title: Analytics & Reports
reports.description: Deliver consistent dashboards, scheduled reports, forecasts, anomalies, and decision narratives.

experiments.title: Growth Experiments
experiments.description: Register hypotheses, assign variants, guard metrics, evaluate results, and promote learning.

intelligence.title: Market & Competitive Intelligence
intelligence.description: Monitor demand, competitors, funding, hiring, news, regulation, events, and opportunities.

runs.title: Runs
runs.description: Observe queued and long-running work, approvals, retries, failures, cost, lineage, and cancellation.

workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.description: Design deterministic versioned workflows with policies, approvals, retries, and compensation.

agents.title: Agents & Templates
agents.description: Manage specialized agent roles, versions, templates, instructions, tools, evaluation, cost, and safety.

marketplace.title: Templates & Agent Market
marketplace.description: Discover, inspect, install, version, and safely extend workflows, agents, and templates.

connectors.title: Integrations
connectors.description: Configure versioned channel, data, storage, communication, and MCP integrations.

models.title: Models & Routing
models.description: Route work by quality, language, latency, cost, context, policy, and availability.

developer.title: Developer Platform
developer.description: Build with APIs, webhooks, service accounts, schemas, SDKs, events, and extension contracts.

team.title: Workspaces & Access
team.description: Manage organizations, workspaces, members, teams, roles, permissions, invitations, and reviews.

compliance.title: Compliance & Security
compliance.description: Control consent, suppression, privacy, retention, residency, secrets, and high-risk actions.

audit.title: Audit Log
audit.description: Trace human and agent actions, approvals, tools, configuration changes, and security events.

settings.title: System Settings
settings.description: Configure workspace identity, locale, time zone, currencies, notifications, and operations.

capabilities.capabilitiesIntro: The complete domain remains visible here. Reserved capabilities are architectural commitments, not claims of current operability.
capabilities.domainContractBody: Canonical objects, ownership, permissions, and route identity are defined.
capabilities.deliveryStageBody: This capability belongs to delivery phase {phase}.
capabilities.accessBoundaryBody: The navigation and future permissions target the {audience} experience.
capabilities.currentBoundaryIntro: This page reports capability status; it does not simulate production operations.
capabilities.foundationBoundary: The shared data and API foundation is being implemented and verified. Operational workflows, external integrations, and measurable downstream outcomes will only be enabled when their real services and controls exist.

businessTruth.recordsDescription: Every record has a stable ID, immutable versions, business purpose, actor lineage, and workspace boundary.
businessTruth.emptyBody: Create the first governed record for this capability. It will be written through the workspace-authorized API with audit and outbox lineage.
businessTruth.name: Name
businessTruth.payloadHelp: Store domain attributes only. Do not include credentials, secrets, or conflicting copies of shared objects.
businessTruth.referenceEmpty: Create the required canonical object first, then return to this form.
businessTruth.preservedAttributes: {count, plural, one {# additional attribute from the previous version will be preserved} other {# additional attributes from the previous version will be preserved}}.
businessTruth.committeeRowsPlaceholder: Decision maker | high influence | ROI and delivery risk
businessTruth.outcomeRowsPlaceholder: Metric | result | measurement period | evidence note
businessTruth.retrievalPlaceholder: Search names, purpose, content, findings, or claims
businessTruth.retrievalSearchLabel: Search loaded knowledge and evidence records
businessTruth.retrievalBoundary: {count, plural, one {# loaded workspace record matches} other {# loaded workspace records match}}. Semantic retrieval and chunk generation remain a later pipeline.
businessTruth.noRetrievalResultsBody: Adjust the search or record-type filter. No substitute or generated results have been inserted.
businessTruth.citationsHelp: Each claim points to one immutable evidence version
businessTruth.citationEvidenceHelp: The saved citation remains pinned to this version even when newer evidence is created.
businessTruth.citationClaimHelp: Write only the claim supported by the selected evidence and locator.
businessTruth.citationLocatorHelp: Identify the exact section, page, URL, or record where the support can be checked.
businessTruth.citationCustomLocatorHelp: Enter a JSON object only when a compound legacy locator must be preserved.
businessTruth.noCitations: No evidence citation has been added to this version.
businessTruth.noEvidenceForCitation: Create a governed evidence record before adding citations.

industry.label: Industry
industry.help: The primary industry the brand serves.

website.label: Primary website
website.help: The canonical public website URL, if available.

positioning.label: Brand positioning
positioning.help: Who the brand serves, the category it competes in, and why it is distinct.

mission.label: Mission
mission.help: The enduring outcome the organization exists to create.

primaryAudience.label: Primary audience
primaryAudience.help: Describe the principal buyer or stakeholder audience.

voiceTraits.label: Voice traits
voiceTraits.help: One approved brand voice trait per line.

valuePropositions.label: Value propositions
valuePropositions.help: One governed customer value proposition per line.

category.label: Product category
category.help: The market-recognized category for this product.

lifecycleStage.label: Lifecycle stage
lifecycleStage.help: Controls whether the product is still being developed, active, or retired.

summary.label: Business summary
summary.help: A factual summary suitable for reuse by downstream growth workflows.

targetCustomers.label: Target customers
targetCustomers.help: One customer segment or use case per line.

coreCapabilities.label: Core capabilities
coreCapabilities.help: One verified product capability per line.

differentiators.label: Differentiators
differentiators.help: One evidence-ready differentiator per line; avoid unsupported claims.

productId.label: Canonical product
productId.help: Links the offer to one governed product ID instead of copying product data.

targetMarket.label: Target market
targetMarket.help: The intended market, region, or customer segment for this offer.

commercialModel.label: Commercial model
commercialModel.help: How customers commercially acquire or consume the offer.

availability.label: Availability
availability.help: The current commercial availability of this offer.

deliverables.label: Offer deliverables
deliverables.help: One included deliverable or entitlement per line.

currency.label: Currency
currency.help: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code, such as EUR or USD.

market.label: Applicable market
market.help: The market or region where this price book applies.

priceBasis.label: Price basis
priceBasis.help: The governance role of these prices in commercial decisions.

offerId.label: Canonical offer
offerId.help: Optionally restrict this price book to a governed offer ID.

validFrom.label: Valid from
validFrom.help: The first date these prices may be used.

validTo.label: Valid to
validTo.help: Optional final date these prices may be used.

priceEntries.label: Price entries
priceEntries.help: One entry per line: item reference | numeric amount | billing unit.

countryCode.label: Country code
countryCode.help: Two-letter ISO 3166-1 code, such as DE or US.

region.label: Region
region.help: Optional commercial region or subnational focus.

languages.label: Market languages
languages.help: One supported or required language code per line.

marketStatus.label: Market status
marketStatus.help: The governed stage of market evaluation or operation.

priority.label: Market priority
priority.help: How this market ranks in current go-to-market allocation.

entryMode.label: Entry model
entryMode.help: The primary route used to enter and serve this market.

channelAvailability.label: Available channels
channelAvailability.help: One viable owned, partner, sales, media, or marketplace channel per line.

localizationRequirements.label: Localization requirements
localizationRequirements.help: One language, content, product, support, or commercial adaptation per line.

regulatoryNotes.label: Regulatory notes
regulatoryNotes.help: Known legal, privacy, product, tax, or industry constraints; do not treat assumptions as legal advice.

assumptions.label: Market assumptions
assumptions.help: One testable market-entry assumption per line.

marketId.label: Canonical market
marketId.help: Links this ICP to one governed market ID rather than copying market data.

companySize.label: Company size
companySize.help: The primary organization-size segment for this profile.

employeeRange.label: Employee range
employeeRange.help: Optional human-readable employee range, such as 200–1,000.

revenueRange.label: Revenue range
revenueRange.help: Optional qualified revenue range including its currency and period.

targetAccounts.label: Reference accounts
targetAccounts.help: One fictional or approved representative account pattern per line; do not insert private prospect data.

pains.label: Business pains
pains.help: One evidence-ready customer pain per line.

triggers.label: Buying triggers
triggers.help: One observable event that can create buying urgency per line.

useCases.label: Priority use cases
useCases.help: One product or service use case per line.

buyingCommittee.label: Buying committee
buyingCommittee.help: One role per line: role | influence | priorities.

qualificationCriteria.label: Qualification criteria
qualificationCriteria.help: One condition that strengthens fit per line.

exclusions.label: Exclusion criteria
exclusions.help: One condition that disqualifies or deprioritizes an account per line.

evidenceType.label: Evidence type
evidenceType.help: Classify the source without overstating its verification level.

sourceTitle.label: Source title
sourceTitle.help: The exact title of the source record, document, interview, or dataset.

sourceUrl.label: Source URL
sourceUrl.help: Optional authorized or public source URL; never enter credential-bearing links.

publisher.label: Publisher or owner
publisher.help: The organization or internal owner responsible for the source.

publishedAt.label: Published date
publishedAt.help: When the source was originally published, if known.

collectedAt.label: Collected date
collectedAt.help: When this evidence entered the governed workspace record.

sourceLocale.label: Source language
sourceLocale.help: The language or locale of the underlying source content.

verificationStatus.label: Verification status
verificationStatus.help: Distinguishes verified, owner-attested, third-party, and unverified evidence.

reliability.label: Reliability assessment
reliability.help: A governed assessment that remains separate from the source itself.

usageRights.label: Usage rights
usageRights.help: How Grovello may store and reuse this source inside the workspace.

scope.label: Evidence scope
scope.help: State the market, product, period, sample, or conditions this evidence actually covers.

evidenceSummary.label: Evidence summary
evidenceSummary.help: A factual summary that does not expand beyond the source.

limitations.label: Limitations
limitations.help: One caveat, uncertainty, expiry condition, or exclusion per line.

reviewAt.label: Review date
reviewAt.help: Optional date when an owner should re-check accuracy and currency.

documentType.label: Document type
documentType.help: The governed role this document serves in the knowledge system.

canonicalObjectId.label: Canonical subject
canonicalObjectId.help: Optionally link the document to one governed brand, product, offer, market, or ICP ID.

ownerTeam.label: Owning team
ownerTeam.help: The team accountable for accuracy, review, and retirement.

knowledgeStatus.label: Knowledge status
knowledgeStatus.help: Whether the content is working material, approved for reuse, or superseded.

documentSummary.label: Document summary
documentSummary.help: A concise factual synopsis for operators and retrieval results.

knowledgeBody.label: Governed content
knowledgeBody.help: The approved document body for this version; never include credentials or private customer data.

topics.label: Topics
topics.help: One governed topic or taxonomy term per line.

retrievalKeywords.label: Retrieval keywords
retrievalKeywords.help: One exact term, product name, question pattern, or synonym per line.

audiences.label: Approved audiences
audiences.help: One role, market, or customer audience per line.

caseStudyType.label: Case-study type
caseStudyType.help: Classify the governed customer, partner, pilot, transformation, or internal validation story.

disclosureStatus.label: Disclosure status
disclosureStatus.help: Controls whether the case may be public, anonymized, confidential, or used only as a fictional fixture.

customerDisplayName.label: Approved customer name
customerDisplayName.help: Use only an authorized public name, approved anonymized label, or clearly fictional fixture name.

customerIndustry.label: Customer industry
customerIndustry.help: The industry context needed to interpret the case without copying a private customer profile.

icpId.label: Canonical ICP
icpId.help: Optionally link the case to one governed ideal customer profile.

engagementStartedAt.label: Engagement started
engagementStartedAt.help: The date the measured implementation, pilot, or engagement began.

engagementEndedAt.label: Engagement ended
engagementEndedAt.help: Optional date the measured period ended; leave blank for an ongoing engagement.

caseSummary.label: Case summary
caseSummary.help: A factual synopsis of the governed case and its applicable context.

challenge.label: Customer challenge
challenge.help: Describe the observed problem without inventing customer intent or confidential details.

approach.label: Approach
approach.help: Describe the implemented product, service, workflow, and responsibilities.

outcomes.label: Measured outcomes
outcomes.help: One outcome per line: metric | result | measurement period | evidence note. Active cases also require exact evidence citations.

lessons.label: Lessons learned
lessons.help: One reusable, evidence-bounded lesson per line.

approvedUseCases.label: Approved uses
approvedUseCases.help: One channel, audience, enablement, or knowledge use authorized for this case per line.

authorizationReference.label: Authorization reference
authorizationReference.help: Record the consent, contract, approval, or fictional-fixture reference that permits this use.

fieldErrors.currency: Currency must be a three-letter code such as EUR or USD.
fieldErrors.countryCode: Country code must contain two letters, such as DE or US.
fieldErrors.dateOrder: The valid-to date cannot be earlier than the valid-from date.
fieldErrors.engagementDateOrder: The engagement end date cannot be earlier than its start date.
fieldErrors.priceRow: Price entry line {line} must use: item reference | amount | billing unit.
fieldErrors.priceAmount: Price entry line {line} must contain a non-negative numeric amount.
fieldErrors.committeeRow: Buying committee line {line} must use: role | influence | priorities.
fieldErrors.outcomeRow: Outcome line {line} must use: metric | result | measurement period | evidence note.
fieldErrors.citationEvidence: Evidence citation {line} must select an exact evidence version.
fieldErrors.citationClaim: Evidence citation {line} must contain the supported claim.
fieldErrors.citationLocator: Evidence citation {line} must contain a checkable locator.
fieldErrors.citationLocatorJson: Evidence citation {line} uses an invalid custom locator; enter a JSON object.

businessTruth.payloadObjectRequired: The structured payload must be a JSON object.
businessTruth.invalidPayload: The structured payload is not valid JSON.
businessTruth.createdNotice: Created {name} with audit and outbox lineage.
businessTruth.versionedNotice: Saved a new immutable version of {name}.
businessTruth.unavailableBody: Start the local API and database, or configure the authorized Grovello API connection. No fallback data has been substituted.
businessTruth.unauthorizedBody: This session does not have permission to read or change shared business truth in the selected workspace.
businessTruth.conflictBody: A record with this type and stable slug already exists. Use a new version for changes.
businessTruth.unknownError: The record could not be saved.

onboarding.startedNotice: Business Setup has started and can be resumed from this workspace.
onboarding.activatedNotice: The exact business-profile snapshot is active.
onboarding.loadingBody: Reading the persistent checklist and canonical profile from the workspace API.
onboarding.unauthorizedBody: This session cannot read or operate Business Setup in the selected workspace.
onboarding.unavailableBody: The authorized workspace API is unavailable. No simulated completion state is shown.
onboarding.emptyBody: Choose the canonical facts this workspace must complete before strategy and execution can rely on them.
onboarding.checklistBody: Each item links to its canonical editor; this checklist stores evidence of completion, not private data copies.
onboarding.factMissing: A canonical record is still required.
onboarding.gapsBody: Blocking gaps come from deterministic validation of canonical facts and references.
onboarding.gapFallback: Resolve this validation gap before activation.
onboarding.noGapsBody: The activation gate still verifies permissions, policy, exact versions, and required workspace settings.
onboarding.activationBody: Activation is separate from import completion and pins an exact version snapshot.
onboarding.activationBlocked: Submitting runs the server-side completeness, policy, permission, and exact-version gate. Any blocking gaps are persisted for recovery.
onboarding.activeBody: Activation version {version} is now the governed input for downstream workflows.
onboarding.conflictBody: The profile changed or does not satisfy the activation gate. Refresh and resolve the reported gaps.
onboarding.unknownError: Business Setup could not complete the request.

imports.loadingBody: Reading workspace-scoped jobs and durable workflow state.
imports.detailLoadingBody: Reading the selected job, validation report, and immutable change set.
imports.unauthorizedBody: This session does not have permission to operate imports in the selected workspace.
imports.unavailableBody: The API or a required local service is unavailable. No import success is simulated.
imports.jobsBody: Every job retains source verification, mapping, validation, approval, apply, and recovery lineage.
imports.emptyBody: Upload an allowed UTF-8 CSV or versioned Grovello JSON package.
imports.selectBody: Choose a job to inspect its durable state and available governed actions.
imports.chooseFile: Choose a CSV or Grovello JSON file
imports.fileHelp: UTF-8 only · 25 MiB maximum · no archives, macros, remote URLs, or executable mappings
imports.fileTooLarge: The selected file exceeds the 25 MiB import limit.
imports.unsupportedSource: Choose a .csv or .json file with an allowed content type.
imports.invalidSourceFields: The source fields could not be detected. Verify the CSV header or JSON records.
imports.uploadStartedNotice: {name} was uploaded; verification and malware scanning have started.
imports.changeSetCreatedNotice: Immutable change set version {version} was created.
imports.approvedNotice: The reviewed change set was approved.
imports.rejectedNotice: The reviewed change set was rejected.
imports.cancelledNotice: Cancellation was recorded and sent to the active workflow.
imports.failureFallback: The workflow stopped with a classified failure.
imports.mappingBody: Mappings are immutable, deterministic, and cannot execute code or network calls.
imports.sourceFieldsHelp: Comma-separated exact source selectors. Re-enter them when resuming a mapping on another browser.
imports.validationReadyBody: Run bounded parsing, normalization, canonical reference checks, and exact deduplication.
imports.reviewBody: {valid} valid rows and {invalid} rows requiring attention.
imports.validationCleanBody: Review normalized previews before creating the immutable change set.
imports.compensationBody: Compensation creates corrective or archived versions and stops on concurrent owner edits.
imports.unknownError: The import request could not be completed.
imports.conflictBody: The import state or canonical versions changed. Refresh and review before retrying.
imports.validationErrorBody: The API rejected this import request: {detail}

processing.verifying: Verifying the exact uploaded object version and checksum.
processing.scanning: Scanning the private source before parsing.
processing.validating: Parsing and validating within configured resource limits.
processing.applying: Writing canonical immutable versions in bounded transactions.

assets.loadingBody: Reading canonical Asset versions and safe file bindings from the workspace API.
assets.searchPlaceholder: Search by asset name or stable slug
assets.approvalBoundary: Active assets require asset approval permission
assets.libraryBody: Canonical metadata and immutable file versions available to this workspace.
assets.noResultsBody: Change the search text to return records already loaded from the workspace API.
assets.noBoundFile: This version has no governed original-file binding.
assets.downloadUnavailable: Only active, clean, available versions can be downloaded.
assets.loadingVersionsBody: Resolving exact immutable versions and safe file metadata.
assets.selectAssetBody: Choose an Asset record to inspect its immutable version history.
assets.emptyBody: Upload a supported file, verify and scan it, then finalize it as a canonical Asset version.
assets.chooseFile: Choose or drop a supported file
assets.fileHelp: JPEG, PNG, WebP, or PDF · maximum 100 MB
assets.targetAssetHelp: Select an existing Asset only when this file is its next immutable version.
assets.unsupportedType: Use a JPEG, PNG, WebP, or PDF file.
assets.fileTooLarge: The selected file exceeds the 100 MB local profile limit.
assets.unknownError: The Asset operation could not be completed.
assets.activeApprovalNote: Active finalization invokes the asset.approve permission boundary and makes a clean version eligible for governed download.
assets.draftApprovalNote: Draft finalization preserves the immutable version but does not authorize download.
assets.downloadIssued: Issued a short-lived private download for {name}.
assets.finalizedNotice: Finalized {name} as an immutable Asset version.

terminal.quarantined: The file failed malware scanning and cannot become an Asset.
terminal.failed: The workflow failed closed. Start a new upload after reviewing the failure.
terminal.expired: The signed upload window expired. Start a new upload.
terminal.cancelled: The durable upload workflow was cancelled.

dashboard.summary: Your growth system is on track. Two decisions need review before the next execution window.
dashboard.growthLoopDescription: Global B2B Growth · one measurable path from business onboarding to retained revenue and the next strategy cycle.
dashboard.decisionDescription: Every recommendation requires evidence, expected impact, and an approval policy.
dashboard.decisionOneBody: Create a German comparison hub and prioritize five evidence-backed product claims.
dashboard.decisionTwo: Industrial automation accounts show an active buying signal
dashboard.decisionTwoBody: Launch a 14-day account sequence for 42 verified buying-committee contacts after compliance review.
dashboard.performanceDescription: Attributed and assisted revenue by operating channel.
dashboard.executionDescription: Current workflow activity. Values are seed data until services are connected.

architecture.title: One shared context. Many specialized agents. One growth loop.
architecture.intro: Grovello is an Enterprise Growth OS. Global Go-to-Market & Revenue Growth is its international capability, and Global B2B Growth is the first golden acceptance journey.
architecture.positionProductBody: The product serves domestic and international enterprise growth; it is not limited to one industry or export business.
architecture.positionCapabilityBody: Market entry, acquisition, pipeline, revenue, retention, and learning share one business truth.
architecture.positionJourneyBody: The first golden journey proves the full loop; industrial automation is replaceable reference data.
architecture.sharedContextBody: Brand, products, knowledge, ICP, customers, consent, commercial facts, and revenue outcomes use canonical identities.
architecture.controlPlaneBody: Goals, budgets, policy, Temporal workflow state, approvals, idempotency, audit, and result feedback.
architecture.agentPlaneBody: LangGraph strategy, content, SEO, GEO, video, research, outbound, sales, success, analytics, and compliance agents.
architecture.connectorPlaneBody: Versioned APIs, webhooks, MCP tools, controlled browser fallback, model providers, storage, and channels.
architecture.outcomePlaneBody: Touchpoints, opportunities, orders, revenue, margin, retention, experiments, attribution, insights, and next-cycle recommendations.
architecture.rulesBody: Architecture decisions that prevent the platform from drifting into disconnected AI tools.
architecture.ruleOne: PostgreSQL remains the business source of truth; caches, indexes, and analytics projections are rebuildable.
architecture.ruleTwo: Temporal owns durable business state; LangGraph owns model reasoning state.
architecture.ruleThree: High-risk actions pass authorization, policy, consent, and approval before connector execution.
architecture.ruleFour: Model, identity, storage, and channel providers remain replaceable through public contracts.

journeys.title: Global B2B Growth
journeys.intro: Operate one governed path from business onboarding and international market entry through acquisition, sales, revenue, retention, and the next AI strategy cycle.
journeys.progressBody: The structure is defined; external providers and downstream commercial truth are not connected yet.
journeys.acceptanceBody: The journey is complete only when every downstream result is real, linked, governed, and measurable.
journeys.rule1: Replace the industry, origin, market, language, and currency through data—never by changing the domain model.
journeys.rule2: Revenue and margin come from commercial records or connected systems, never model inference.
journeys.rule3: A page, prompt, connector manifest, or successful demo call is not an operational capability.
journeys.decisionsBody: The architecture is fixed; external access and legal authorization remain explicit owner inputs.
journeys.decisionConnectorsBody: Supply authorized accounts, credentials, budgets, and optional real business data when enabling providers.

deliveryRoadmap.title: Grovello delivery roadmap
deliveryRoadmap.intro: Follow the dependency-ordered path from product foundations to a self-hostable, extensible Enterprise Growth OS. A stage advances only when its completion gate is supported by real system evidence.
deliveryRoadmap.sequenceBody: Later domains remain in scope from day one, but their completion gates cannot bypass unmet upstream dependencies.
deliveryRoadmap.verifiedBoundaryBody: The repository baseline, product constitution, bilingual console, architecture boundaries, and capability status are verified.
deliveryRoadmap.earliestGateBody: Foundation models and isolation exist; persistent identity, administrator flows, and the complete recovery gate remain open.
deliveryRoadmap.currentFocusBody: Brand and product administration is being deepened while the remaining profile, import, retrieval, and evidence requirements stay explicit.
deliveryRoadmap.stagesBody: Expand a stage to see what must be delivered, the evidence required to leave it, and its upstream dependency.
deliveryRoadmap.dependencyNone: Baseline stage; no earlier delivery dependency.
deliveryRoadmap.dependencyPhase: Depends on the completion gate for Phase {phase}; parallel foundation work is allowed without claiming completion.
deliveryRoadmap.principleBody: Seed data, mock providers, a page, a manifest, or one successful demo may support development, but none can satisfy an operational completion gate unless the roadmap explicitly allows controlled test evidence.

0.title: Product engineering foundation
0.outcome: Create an executable product and engineering baseline.
0.deliverables: Product constitution, bilingual console, domain navigation, API/workflow/agent/connector boundaries, and capability status.
0.gate: CI passes; seed data is explicitly labeled; no external capability is fabricated.

1.title: Organization & access
1.deliverables: Organization, workspace, user, team, role, policy, session, audit, tenant isolation, administrator, and recovery flows.
1.gate: Authorized, unauthorized, tenant-isolation, audit, recovery, and administrator paths all pass.

2.title: Shared business truth
2.outcome: Every agent uses one governed set of facts.
2.deliverables: Brand, product, offer, price book, market, ICP, evidence, knowledge, assets, case studies, import, retrieval, and version management.
2.gate: A complete business profile can be created, validated, versioned, retrieved, and cited without conflicting copies.

3.title: Goals & governance
3.outcome: Every execution has measurable purpose and authority.
3.deliverables: Goals, budgets, KPI definitions, approval matrix, consent, unsubscribe and suppression, risk levels, and cost limits.
3.gate: Every external action resolves purpose, owner, budget, policy, approval, and measurable outcome.

4.title: Connector platform
4.outcome: Providers can change without changing domain logic.
4.deliverables: Registry, manifests, auth references, capability and risk contracts, health, rate limits, webhooks, idempotency, sandboxes, official APIs, and browser fallback rules.
4.gate: Representative read, write, webhook, failure, retry, revocation, and audit tests pass through one versioned contract.

5.title: Traffic engine
5.deliverables: Content, sites and pages, SEO, GEO, video, publishing, social operations, advertising, creative assets, and experiment variants.
5.gate: Battle plans become published or live assets; exposure, visits, engagement, cost, variants, and source lineage return as unified events.

6.title: Lead intelligence
6.outcome: Anonymous demand and target accounts become governed leads.
6.deliverables: Visitor and account identification, account discovery, contact enrichment, verification, deduplication, provenance, legal basis, and scoring.
6.gate: A lead is source- and purpose-traceable, deduplicated, policy-compliant, scored, and linked to account and contact identities.

7.title: Outreach & conversations
7.deliverables: Email, supported multichannel sequences, booking, unified inbox, reply classification, unsubscribe, bounce, suppression, and human takeover.
7.gate: Approved sequences send, receive, and classify replies, honor suppression, build a timeline, and stop or hand off safely.

8.title: CRM & opportunities
8.outcome: Qualified conversations enter a managed sales pipeline.
8.deliverables: Lifecycle, assignment, tasks, meetings, qualification, opportunity stages, forecasting, and AI sales assistance.
8.gate: A qualified lead becomes an opportunity with owner, history, next step, forecast, evidence, and win or loss reason preserved.

9.title: Commerce & revenue
9.deliverables: Quotes, discount approval, contracts, orders, invoices, payments, currencies, tax references, revenue, and margin events.
9.gate: One opportunity reaches reconciled payment and revenue through governed records; models never invent financial facts.

10.title: Customer growth
10.outcome: Revenue continues into durable customer value.
10.deliverables: Onboarding, milestones, adoption, health, support context, renewal or repeat purchase, expansion, advocacy, and referral.
10.gate: A customer has an owner, success plan, health evidence, risk actions, and a measured retention, expansion, or referral outcome.

11.title: Data & intelligence
11.outcome: The system can explain growth performance.
11.deliverables: Identity graph, event taxonomy, metric registry, lineage, attribution, cost, margin, experiments, reports, and data quality.
11.gate: Revenue can be repeatedly attributed with uncertainty; reports reconcile to source events and expose data quality.

12.title: AI decision loop
12.outcome: Real evidence changes the next action.
12.deliverables: Decision context, recommendations, plan generation, scenarios, evaluation, budget reallocation, approval, and rollback.
12.gate: The system proposes a new plan from real results, explains evidence and uncertainty, passes policy, and records acceptance or rejection.

13.title: Open-source release
13.outcome: Third parties can run and extend the real core.
13.deliverables: License decision, installation, migrations, seed data, extension SDK and contracts, security docs, upgrade and rollback, contribution, and release flows.
13.gate: A clean environment self-hosts the golden journey, replaces providers, and upgrades safely without an author-controlled private service.

module.definedBody: The route, shared objects, business outcome, and governance boundary are part of the formal architecture.
module.workflowBody: Durable state and approval policy will be delivered through versioned Temporal workflows.
module.connectionBody: No production provider is configured in this new local foundation.
module.lifecycleDescription: Every capability follows a governed path and writes measurable outcomes back to the system.
module.sharedObjectsDescription: This module references canonical objects instead of creating isolated copies.
module.businessOutcomeBody: Evidence returns to the same revenue and learning model.
module.recordsDescription: Seed records illustrate table density and workflow states.
module.settingsBody: Local placeholders make configuration boundaries explicit before providers are connected.
module.writeAuditBody: Record human, agent, workflow, and connector decisions.
module.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the intended business outcome and constraints.
module.saveDraftApproval: Save as draft and require human approval before execution
module.noLiveDataBody: Connect the API and required providers before this module can execute production work.

auth.title: Orchestrate every growth loop.
auth.body: Shared context, specialized agents, durable workflows, connected channels, and revenue feedback in one operating system.
auth.loginBody: Sign in to your Grovello workspace.
auth.registerBody: Start with a self-hosted growth operating system.
auth.forgotBody: Enter your email to request recovery instructions.
auth.authPending: The production identity provider is not connected in this foundation build.
auth.terms: Configure privacy, security, and acceptable-use policies before production use.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page may have moved or is not part of this workspace.
